verses.text: Ezra's lineage is traced back through the high priests to Aaron. He comes of a true priestly line. God prepares and credentials the servant He will use, rooting him in a heritage of faithful service.
Ezra's lineage is traced back through the high priests to Aaron. He comes of a true priestly line. God prepares and credentials the servant He will use, rooting him in a heritage of faithful service.
Ezra's lineage is traced back through the high priests to Aaron. He comes of a true priestly line. God prepares and credentials the servant He will use, rooting him in a heritage of faithful service.
Ezra's lineage is traced back through the high priests to Aaron. He comes of a true priestly line. God prepares and credentials the servant He will use, rooting him in a heritage of faithful service.
Ezra's lineage is traced back through the high priests to Aaron. He comes of a true priestly line. God prepares and credentials the servant He will use, rooting him in a heritage of faithful service.

Ezra, 'a ready scribe in the law of Moses,' goes up with the king granting all he asked, 'for the hand of the LORD his God was upon him,' since he had set his heart to seek, do, and teach the law. Devotion to Scripture made him fit for usefulness. God's hand rests on the one who first prepares his own heart.

Devotion to Scripture made him fit for usefulness. God's hand rests on the one who first prepares his own heart.
Artaxerxes' letter grants Ezra leave to take all who freely will to Jerusalem. The king opens the door wide. God can make rulers eager to forward the very work the world once opposed.

Ezra is sent with the king's silver and gold and the freewill offerings to buy what is needed for God's house. Royal and willing gifts flow toward the temple. When God stirs hearts, both kings and common people give to His work.

Ezra is sent with the king's silver and gold and the freewill offerings to buy what is needed for God's house. Royal and willing gifts flow toward the temple. When God stirs hearts, both kings and common people give to His work.

Ezra is sent with the king's silver and gold and the freewill offerings to buy what is needed for God's house. Royal and willing gifts flow toward the temple. When God stirs hearts, both kings and common people give to His work.

The king bids Ezra spend the funds on sacrifices and whatever else seems good, drawing more from the treasury as required. He is trusted with generous liberty. God provides amply for the work He commissions, often through unexpected hands.

Artaxerxes orders the treasurers beyond the river to supply Ezra's needs and exempts the temple ministers from tax. The king secures the work and honours its servants. Those who serve God's house are cared for under His providence.

Ezra is charged to appoint magistrates who know God's law, to teach it, and to enforce it on the disobedient. Authority is given to establish God's word in the land. Reform takes both teaching and just government to take root.

Ezra is charged to appoint magistrates who know God's law, to teach it, and to enforce it on the disobedient. Authority is given to establish God's word in the land. Reform takes both teaching and just government to take root.

Ezra blesses the LORD who put this in the king's heart and extended mercy to him, and is strengthened for the task. He traces every favour to God, not to himself. The faithful servant meets opportunity with thanksgiving and fresh courage.
